rubrics: The response operates in MODE B (pure cost analysis)
The response sets income change to $0 for all household profiles
The response does NOT mention wage ripple, wage compression, or wage increase as a benefit
The response does NOT claim any household is better off or a winner
The response frames the impact as a pure cost increase borne by consumers
The response includes pass-through rates specific to material substitution or packaging cost policies
The response labels the distributional burden as regressive or proportional, NOT progressive
The scorecard shows only cost changes, not net income gains
The response does NOT fabricate an income effect that does not exist in the policy
